prompt.pick_skippable: Pick a number [{range}, Enter to skip]:
init.language.header: gmlw: first run — which language should I speak to you?
init.name.header: gmlw: what should I call you?
init.role.intro: Your role is the functional hat you wear — the kind of work you do. It's a lens over who you are, not a copy of it. Pick one, or type your own.
init.role.header: gmlw: which role are you working as?
init.role.eg.engineer.label: Software engineer
init.role.eg.po.label: Product owner
init.role.eg.po.desc: Shaping what gets built, and why.
init.role.eg.qa.label: QA engineer
init.role.eg.writer.label: Technical writer
init.environment.intro: Your environment is the place or context you work in — work, home, an open-source project — carrying that setting's people, systems, and norms. Pick one, or type your own.
init.environment.header: gmlw: where does this work happen?
init.env.eg.work.label: Work
init.env.eg.home.label: Home
init.env.eg.oss.label: Open source
init.env.eg.personal.label: Personal project
init.env.eg.personal.desc: A dedicated project of your own.
init.client.header: gmlw: which client should I wrap by default?
init.client.offer_update: gmlw: {display} is behind ({installed} → {latest}). Update it now?
init.install.header: gmlw: which client do you want to install? (pick the one you pay for)
init.install.copied: (copied to your clipboard — just paste and run)
init.install.self: I'll run it myself in another terminal
init.install.run_failed: gmlw: the install command exited with code {code} — you can finish it yourself.
init.install.waiting: gmlw: waiting for {display} to appear on your PATH…
init.install.recheck_header: gmlw: {display} isn't on your PATH yet.
init.install.gaveup: gmlw: {display} still not detected — run gmlw again once it's installed.
init.prereq.needed: gmlw: {display} is needed first — let's install it.
init.persona.header: gmlw: pick a persona (its voice greets you and can shape tone)?
init.announce.fresh: gmlw: set up — speaking {language}, calling you {name}, role '{role}' ({role_slug}) on '{environment}' ({environment_slug}). Change any of it in ~/.gmlw/config.toml.
init.announce.legacy: gmlw: your choices were saved into ~/.gmlw/config.toml — your other settings and comments are untouched.
init.announce.client: gmlw: default client '{client}'. Change it in ~/.gmlw/config.toml.
init.announce.no_client: gmlw: no supported client found on your PATH (claude, cursor-agent, codex, vibe). Install one or set [client] default in ~/.gmlw/config.toml.
init.announce.persona: gmlw: persona '{persona}' selected — it will greet you at launch.
jobs.none: No jobs yet. Start one with: gmlw start <job>
sessions.none: No sessions for {job}. Start one with: gmlw start {start_job}
workflow.none: No workflows yet. Author one with: gmlw workflow new <name>
workflow.new.exists:
gmlw: a workflow named '{name}' already exists.
  To change it, use:  gmlw workflow edit {name}
workflow.new.deployed: gmlw: workflow '{name}' created. Run it with:  gmlw run {name}
workflow.new.incomplete:
gmlw: the workflow wasn't finished, so nothing was deployed.
  Your draft is kept at {draft} — run gmlw workflow new again to pick it back up.
guided.header: gmlw: how do you want to shape this workflow? (creating it well makes every run smoother)
guided.choice_guided: guided — a facilitative guide walks you through it (richer, costs a bit more)
plugins.none: No plugins installed. Add one at ~/.gmlw/plugins/<id>/ (with a plugin.toml).
migration.moved: gmlw: migrated {count} item(s) from profile/company into environments/{environment}: {items}.
migration.skipped: gmlw: left {count} item(s) in profile/company (already present in environments/{environment}): {items}.
migration.slugs: gmlw: tidied {count} role/environment folder name(s) into slugs: {items}.
client.guidance.missing: gmlw: client {client} ({display}) isn't on your PATH yet.
client.guidance.use_other: or use one you already have:  --client {other}
client.guidance.unsupported: gmlw: {client} is not a supported client. Supported: {supported}.
client.guidance.none_installed: No supported client is installed yet — any of these works:
client.guidance.then_login: ...then log in (see each tool's docs), or run `gmlw init` to be guided.
preflight.cwd_gone:
gmlw: your current directory no longer exists (it was moved or deleted).
  cd to a directory that exists — e.g. `cd ~` — and try again.
start.needs_job:
gmlw: start needs a job to work on.
  gmlw <job>          start (or resume) a session on <job>
  gmlw start <job>    the same, spelled out
A job groups related sessions; list yours with:  gmlw jobs
run.pick_header: gmlw: which workflow do you want to run?
run.needs_workflow:
gmlw: run needs a workflow.
  gmlw run <workflow>   run a workflow directly (the job is named after it)
List the runnable ones with:  gmlw workflow list
run.no_workflows: gmlw: no workflows to run yet. Author one with:  gmlw workflow new <name>
config.unknown_key: gmlw: unknown setting '{key}'. See: gmlw config list
index.tagline: gmlw — a companion around your ML coding CLI.
index.group.author: author
index.footer: Next: gmlw help <topic> (job-vs-workflow, start-vs-run, personas, cost), or gmlw --help for every flag.
index.desc.job: start or resume a session on a job
index.desc.run_workflow: run a workflow on the job
index.desc.run: run a workflow directly (the job is named after it)
index.desc.export: report a job's usage and cost
help.unknown: gmlw: no help topic '{topic}'. See: gmlw help
help.job_vs_workflow.summary: how a job differs from a workflow
help.job_vs_workflow.body:
A job is a unit of work you return to — it groups related sessions and their recorded usage (e.g. 'checkout-refactor'). A workflow is a reusable, authored recipe you run on a job (e.g. 'doc-review').
You start sessions on jobs; you run workflows on them.
See: gmlw jobs, gmlw workflow list
help.start_vs_run.summary: starting a session vs running a workflow
help.start_vs_run.body:
gmlw start <job> opens (or resumes) a plain session on a job — the wrapper hands the terminal to your client.
gmlw start <job> -w <workflow> runs an authored workflow on that job instead.
gmlw run <workflow> runs a workflow directly — the job is named after the workflow and its sessions accumulate there. Use it for a recurring procedure; use start for exploratory work on a job.
Bare 'gmlw <job>' is shorthand for 'gmlw start <job>'.
help.personas.body:
A persona is a companion voice: when one is selected it greets you at launch and can shape tone. It is off by default (invisible).
Select one with: gmlw config set companion.persona <name> (see gmlw persona list).
help.cost.summary: where cost is tracked and shown
help.cost.body:
Every session is metered. See a job's usage and cost with gmlw export <job>; the status line shows the running session's cost.
Authoring a workflow is itself a metered session, surfaced alongside the rest.
log.compress_failed: compression failed for {source} ({error}); leaving it uncompressed
log.vibe_no_upstream: could not resolve vibe's active-model upstream; launching unmetered
log.vibe_relay_failed: metering relay failed to start ({error}); launching vibe unmetered
log.relay_failed: metering relay failed to start ({error}); launching unmetered
log.codex_relay_failed: metering relay failed to start ({error}); launching codex unmetered
log.statusline_install_failed: could not install the status line at {path} ({error}); running without it
log.statusline_unreadable: leaving {path} untouched: it is no longer readable JSON
hint.resume: tip: resume a job where you left off — gmlw start <job> --resume-latest
hint.cost: tip: see where cost is tracked — gmlw help cost
hint.persona: tip: pick a companion voice — gmlw persona list
hint.config: tip: view and change settings — gmlw config list
ambient.card:
# gmlw
You are running inside gmlw, a wrapper around this CLI. If the user asks how to do something in gmlw, you can tell them:
- start or resume a session: gmlw start <job> [--resume-latest]
- run a workflow on a job: gmlw start <job> -w <workflow>
- run a workflow directly: gmlw run <workflow>
- see usage and cost: gmlw export <job>
- change settings: gmlw config list, then gmlw config set <key> <value>
- learn a concept: gmlw help <topic>
